prompt: Create a 30-second Manim scene that explains refraction at a flat interface. Show the boundary, a normal line, incident and refracted rays, angle markers, and staged captions so the bending toward the normal is easy to see.
completion:
from manim import *

class OpticsSnellRefractionStory(Scene):
    def construct(self):
        title = Text("Refraction Bends the Ray", font_size=34).to_edge(UP)
        boundary = Line(LEFT * 5.4, RIGHT * 5.4, color=BLUE_B).shift(DOWN * 0.35)
        hit_point = boundary.get_center()
        normal = DashedLine(hit_point + UP * 2.8, hit_point + DOWN * 2.8, color=GRAY_C)
        air_label = Text("air", font_size=24, color=GRAY_D).move_to(LEFT * 4.2 + UP * 1.8)
        water_label = Text("water", font_size=24, color=BLUE_D).move_to(LEFT * 4.0 + DOWN * 1.8)
        incident_guide = Line(hit_point, hit_point + 2.8 * UL, color=BLUE_D)
        refracted_guide = Line(hit_point, hit_point + np.array([1.4, -2.5, 0]), color=GREEN_D)
        incident_ray = Arrow(hit_point + 2.8 * UL, hit_point + 0.08 * UL, buff=0.0, color=BLUE_D, stroke_width=5)
        refracted_ray = Arrow(hit_point + np.array([0.06, -0.1, 0]), hit_point + np.array([1.4, -2.5, 0]), buff=0.0, color=GREEN_D, stroke_width=5)
        normal_up = Line(hit_point, hit_point + UP * 2.2)
        normal_down = Line(hit_point, hit_point + DOWN * 2.2)
        angle_in = Angle(normal_up, incident_guide, radius=0.6, color=BLUE_D)
        angle_out = Angle(refracted_guide, normal_down, radius=0.6, color=GREEN_D)
        theta_in = MathTex(r"\theta_1", color=BLUE_D).next_to(angle_in, LEFT, buff=0.12)
        theta_out = MathTex(r"\theta_2", color=GREEN_D).next_to(angle_out, RIGHT, buff=0.12)
        bend_arrow = CurvedArrow(hit_point + RIGHT * 2.1 + DOWN * 0.4, hit_point + RIGHT * 0.8 + DOWN * 1.3, angle=0.6, color=YELLOW_D)
        caption_a = Text("The incident ray reaches the boundary while the normal gives a reference direction.", font_size=23, color=GRAY_D).to_edge(DOWN)
        caption_b = Text("Inside the slower medium, the refracted ray bends closer to the normal.", font_size=23, color=GRAY_D).to_edge(DOWN)
        caption_c = Text("The smaller second angle is the visual signature of refraction here.", font_size=23, color=GRAY_D).to_edge(DOWN)

        self.play(FadeIn(title), Create(boundary), Create(normal), FadeIn(air_label), FadeIn(water_label), FadeIn(caption_a), run_time=4)
        self.play(GrowArrow(incident_ray), Create(angle_in), FadeIn(theta_in), run_time=4)
        self.play(GrowArrow(refracted_ray), Create(angle_out), FadeIn(theta_out), ReplacementTransform(caption_a, caption_b), run_time=5)
        self.play(Create(bend_arrow), Indicate(angle_out, color=GREEN_A), run_time=4)
        self.play(ReplacementTransform(caption_b, caption_c), Indicate(theta_in, color=BLUE_A), Indicate(theta_out, color=GREEN_A), run_time=4)
        self.wait(2)

prompt: Create a 30-second Manim scene that explains a damped oscillator with a spring-mass sketch on one side and a phase portrait on the other. Use a ValueTracker, a traced phase path, and staged captions so the shrinking amplitude is visually obvious.
completion:
from manim import *

class DampedOscillatorPhasePortraitStory(Scene):
    def construct(self):
        def displacement(t):
            return 1.7 * np.exp(-0.14 * t) * np.cos(2.6 * t)

        def velocity(t):
            return -1.7 * np.exp(-0.14 * t) * (0.14 * np.cos(2.6 * t) + 2.6 * np.sin(2.6 * t))

        title = Text("Damping Shrinks the Oscillator State", font_size=34).to_edge(UP)
        wall = Rectangle(width=0.35, height=2.6, color=GRAY_D, fill_color=GRAY_D, fill_opacity=0.5).move_to(LEFT * 5.6)
        baseline = Line(LEFT * 5.4 + DOWN * 1.55, LEFT * 0.8 + DOWN * 1.55, color=GRAY_C)
        tracker = ValueTracker(0.0)
        mass = always_redraw(lambda: RoundedRectangle(corner_radius=0.1, width=1.05, height=0.82, color=BLUE_D, fill_color=BLUE_E, fill_opacity=0.25).move_to(np.array([-2.2 + displacement(tracker.get_value()), 0.0, 0.0])))
        spring = always_redraw(
            lambda: ParametricFunction(
                lambda s: np.array([
                    interpolate(-5.35, mass.get_left()[0], s),
                    0.18 * np.sin(10 * PI * s),
                    0,
                ]),
                t_range=[0, 1],
                color=YELLOW_D,
                stroke_width=4,
            )
        )
        phase_axes = Axes(x_range=[-2.2, 2.2, 1], y_range=[-4.8, 4.8, 2], x_length=4.7, y_length=4.4, tips=False).to_edge(RIGHT).shift(DOWN * 0.1)
        axis_labels = phase_axes.get_axis_labels(Text("x", font_size=22), Text("v", font_size=22))
        phase_dot = always_redraw(lambda: Dot(phase_axes.c2p(displacement(tracker.get_value()), velocity(tracker.get_value())), color=RED_D, radius=0.08))
        phase_trace = TracedPath(phase_dot.get_center, stroke_color=RED_D, stroke_width=4)
        readout = always_redraw(lambda: VGroup(Text("time", font_size=22, color=GRAY_D), DecimalNumber(tracker.get_value(), num_decimal_places=1, font_size=28, color=YELLOW_D)).arrange(RIGHT, buff=0.15).to_corner(UR).shift(DOWN * 0.7))
        caption_a = Text("The mass oscillates, but damping steadily removes amplitude from the motion.", font_size=23, color=GRAY_D).to_edge(DOWN)
        caption_b = Text("The phase portrait turns that motion into a spiral toward the origin.", font_size=23, color=GRAY_D).to_edge(DOWN)
        caption_c = Text("Both views tell the same story: position and velocity decay together over time.", font_size=23, color=GRAY_D).to_edge(DOWN)

        self.add(phase_trace)
        self.play(FadeIn(title), FadeIn(wall), Create(baseline), Create(phase_axes), FadeIn(axis_labels), FadeIn(caption_a), run_time=4)
        self.play(Create(spring), FadeIn(mass), FadeIn(phase_dot), FadeIn(readout), run_time=4)
        self.play(tracker.animate.set_value(4.0), ReplacementTransform(caption_a, caption_b), run_time=6, rate_func=linear)
        self.play(tracker.animate.set_value(8.0), run_time=6, rate_func=linear)
        self.play(tracker.animate.set_value(11.0), ReplacementTransform(caption_b, caption_c), run_time=5, rate_func=linear)
        self.wait(2)

prompt: Create a 30-second Manim scene that explains magnetic flux through a loop moving across a field region. Show the loop entering, centered, and exiting a highlighted field block, and include a small flux graph or readout with staged captions.
completion:
from manim import *

class MagneticFluxLoopStory(Scene):
    def construct(self):
        def flux_value(x):
            return max(0.0, 1.45 - 0.42 * abs(x))

        title = Text("Flux Peaks When the Loop Sits in the Field", font_size=34).to_edge(UP)
        field_region = Rectangle(width=3.4, height=3.6, color=TEAL_D, fill_color=TEAL_D, fill_opacity=0.14).shift(LEFT * 1.8 + DOWN * 0.1)
        field_symbols = VGroup(*[
            VGroup(Circle(radius=0.12, color=TEAL_D), Dot(color=TEAL_D, radius=0.035)).move_to(field_region.get_corner(UL) + RIGHT * (0.55 + 0.7 * col) + DOWN * (0.55 + 0.7 * row))
            for row in range(4)
            for col in range(4)
        ])
        region_label = Text("magnetic field", font_size=22, color=TEAL_D).next_to(field_region, UP, buff=0.18)
        tracker = ValueTracker(-2.8)
        loop = always_redraw(lambda: Circle(radius=0.62, color=YELLOW_D, stroke_width=5).move_to(np.array([field_region.get_center()[0] + tracker.get_value(), 0.0, 0.0])))
        loop_arrow = always_redraw(lambda: Arrow(loop.get_center() + UP * 1.0, loop.get_center() + UP * 0.18, buff=0.0, color=YELLOW_D, stroke_width=4))
        graph_axes = Axes(x_range=[-3, 3, 1], y_range=[0, 1.8, 0.4], x_length=4.6, y_length=3.0, tips=False).to_edge(RIGHT).shift(DOWN * 0.15)
        graph = graph_axes.plot(flux_value, x_range=[-3, 3], color=BLUE_D)
        graph_point = always_redraw(lambda: Dot(graph_axes.c2p(tracker.get_value(), flux_value(tracker.get_value())), color=BLUE_D, radius=0.08))
        guide = always_redraw(lambda: DashedLine(graph_axes.c2p(tracker.get_value(), 0), graph_point.get_center(), color=BLUE_D, stroke_width=3))
        flux_readout = always_redraw(lambda: VGroup(MathTex(r"\Phi", color=BLUE_D), DecimalNumber(flux_value(tracker.get_value()), num_decimal_places=2, font_size=28, color=BLUE_D)).arrange(RIGHT, buff=0.15).next_to(graph_axes, UP, buff=0.18))
        caption_a = Text("As the loop begins to enter the field region, the linked flux starts to rise.", font_size=23, color=GRAY_D).to_edge(DOWN)
        caption_b = Text("Flux is largest when the loop overlaps the strongest part of the field block.", font_size=23, color=GRAY_D).to_edge(DOWN)
        caption_c = Text("Once the loop leaves the region, the flux falls again and the coupling disappears.", font_size=23, color=GRAY_D).to_edge(DOWN)

        self.play(FadeIn(title), Create(field_region), FadeIn(field_symbols), FadeIn(region_label), Create(graph_axes), Create(graph), FadeIn(caption_a), run_time=4)
        self.play(FadeIn(loop), GrowArrow(loop_arrow), FadeIn(graph_point), Create(guide), FadeIn(flux_readout), tracker.animate.set_value(-0.6), run_time=6)
        self.play(tracker.animate.set_value(0.0), ReplacementTransform(caption_a, caption_b), run_time=5)
        self.play(tracker.animate.set_value(2.8), ReplacementTransform(caption_b, caption_c), run_time=6)
        self.wait(2)
